frame_analyses.description: Setup view of FLS trainer showing Penrose drain, instruments, and marks. No active manipulation yet.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good setup with instruments positioned and suture material visible

frame_analyses.description: Grasper holding needle while positioning for suture loading. Beginning of active task execution.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument grip and needle handling visible

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned near first mark on Penrose drain for initial pass.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful positioning approach, appears to be targeting the marked locations

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement through drain tissue at marked locations.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle control and steady progression through tissue

frame_analyses.description: Completing suture passage through both marks on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both marks appear to be adequately targeted with minimal deviation

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw formation - appears to be creating surgeon's knot with double wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot technique being initiated with appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Continuing surgeon's knot formation with right hand manipulation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good suture control and proper double throw technique visible

frame_analyses.description: Transitioning to second throw with hand switching for proper knot security.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate hand switching observed, maintaining knot tension

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being completed with proper hand switching pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent technique maintained through final throw completion

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Appropriate hand switching and single throw technique
Final throw completed with proper hand switching

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well approximated with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Solid performance with proper surgeon's knot followed by appropriate single throws with hand switching. Good suture placement accuracy and drain closure.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise targeting of marks to minimize deviation penalties
Consider slightly faster needle loading to improve overall time
strengths: Good knot security and tension maintenance
